title: Robotic vehicle for spraying
abstract: There is provided a robotic vehicle (100) for operating in a confined space, such as under a floor of a building, for example a house. The robotic vehicle (100) comprises a chassis (102) having a front (F) and a rear (R) defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front (F) and the rear (R), the robotic vehicle (100) for movement in the longitudinal direction. The robotic vehicle (100) further comprises a plurality of first parts (116a, 116b, 16c, 116d) for a respective plurality of release mechanisms (166), the first parts (116a, 16b, 116c, 116d) each connected to the chassis (102) and each release mechanism (166) for securing a respective wheel (160a, 160b, 160c, 160d) to the chassis (102). Each first part (116a, 116b, 116c, 116d) provides a mounting point for the respective wheel 10 (160a, 160b, 160c, 160d) away from the front (F) or the rear (R) of the chassis (102).Each release mechanism (160) is operable by an operator to release the respective wheel (160a, 160b, 160c, 160d) from the chassis (102) for separate removal of the wheels (160a, 60b, 160c, 160d) and the robotic vehicle (100) from the confined space.
claims:
Claims 1. A robotic vehicle for operating in a confined space, such as under a floor of a building, for example a house, the robotic vehicle comprising: a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the robotic vehicle for movement in the longitudinal direction; a plurality of first parts for a respective plurality of release mechanisms, the first parts each connected to the chassis and each release mechanism for securing a respective wheel to the chassis, wherein each first part provides a mounting point for the respective wheel away from the front or the rear of the chassis, and wherein each release mechanism is operable by an operator to release the respective wheel from the chassis for separate removal of the wheels and the robotic vehicle from the confined space. 2. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising a camera arranged to capture images of the surroundings of the robotic vehicle. 3. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2, further comprising a controller to control operation of the robotic vehicle. 4. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the release mechanism is operable one-handed by an operator to release the respective wheel from the chassis. 5. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the release mechanism is operable tool-less by an operator to release the respective wheel from the chassis. 6. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 5, wherein the release mechanism is a quick- release mechanism. 7. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 6, wherein the release mechanism is a ball detent mechanism. 8. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the robotic vehicle further comprises the wheels, and wherein each wheel comprises a corresponding second part of the release mechanism to be released from the first part of the release mechanism on operation of the release mechanism. 9. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 8, wherein the second part of the release mechanism is configured to be actuated upon one-handed operation by the operator to selectively release the wheel from the chassis. 10. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the wheels are driven wheels to drive the vehicle in the longitudinal direction. 1 1. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein an axis of rotation of the wheels is offset from an axis of movement of the release mechanism during removal of the wheels from the chassis. 12. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 1 1 , wherein the release mechanism is selectively operable to provide one of a plurality of angular positions of the offset between the axis of rotation of the wheels and the axis of movement of the release mechanism. 13. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim for operating over uneven ground, wherein the chassis further comprises: a front chassis portion to connect to one or more front wheels of the vehicle; and a rear chassis portion connected to the front chassis portion at a connection point and to connect to one or more rear wheels of the vehicle, the rear chassis portion being rotatable, at the connection point, relative to the front chassis portion about an axis substantially parallel to the longitudinal direction, the robotic vehicle further comprising: a front-facing sensor provided forward of the connection point, for rotation with the rear chassis portion and substantially directed past the front of the robotic vehicle to detect directional sensor data from in front of the vehicle; and a further sensor for rotation with the rear chassis portion and provided rearward of the connection point, the further sensor to detect further directional sensor data from the vehicle. 14. A robotic vehicle for operating over uneven ground, the robotic vehicle comprising a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the robotic vehicle for movement in the longitudinal direction, the chassis comprising: a front chassis portion to connect to one or more front wheels of the vehicle; and a rear chassis portion connected to the front chassis portion at a connection point and to connect to one or more rear wheels of the vehicle, the rear chassis portion being 

 rotatable, at the connection point, relative to the front chassis portion about an axis substantially parallel to the longitudinal direction, wherein the robotic vehicle further comprises: a front-facing sensor provided forward of the connection point, for rotation with the rear chassis portion and substantially directed past the front of the robotic vehicle to detect directional sensor data from in front of the vehicle; a further sensor for rotation with the rear chassis portion and provided rearward of the connection point, the further sensor to detect further directional sensor data from the vehicle. 15. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the robotic vehicle further comprises a controller arranged to control operation of the robotic vehicle based on a combination of the directional sensor data from the front-facing sensor with the further directional sensor data from the further sensor. 16. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a sensor turret comprising one or more sensors responsive to an environment of the robotic vehicle, secured to the chassis and rotatable about an axis substantially transverse to the longitudinal direction and to an axis of rotation of the wheels, wherein the sensor turret is secured to the chassis by a turret release mechanism operable by an operator, whereby to be removable from the chassis prior to insertion (or removal) of the robotic vehicle to (or from) a confined space. 17. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 16 when dependent on any of claims 13 to 15, wherein the further sensor is one of the one or more sensors of the sensor turret. 18. A robotic vehicle for operating in a confined space, such as under a floor of a building, for example a house, the robotic vehicle comprising: a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the chassis comprising a plurality of wheels each having an axis of rotation substantially transverse to the longitudinal direction for moving the robotic vehicle in the longitudinal direction; a sensor turret comprising one or more sensors responsive to an environment of the robotic vehicle, the sensor turret being secured to the chassis and rotatable about an axis substantially transverse to the longitudinal direction and to the axis of rotation of the wheels, 

 wherein the sensor turret is secured to the chassis by a turret release mechanism operable by an operator, whereby to be removable from the chassis prior to insertion (or removal) of the robotic vehicle to (or from) a confined space. 19. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any of claims 16 to 18, wherein the chassis comprises a first part of the turret release mechanism and the sensor turret comprises a second part of the turret release mechanism to be selectively released from the first part of the turret release mechanism on operation of the turret release mechanism. 20. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 19, wherein the second part of the turret release mechanism is configured to be actuated upon operation by the operator to release the turret from the chassis. 21. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any of claims 16 to 20, wherein the turret release mechanism is a ball detent mechanism. 22. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 21 , wherein the ball detent mechanism is coaxial with the axis of rotation of the sensor turret. 23. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any of claims 16 to 22, wherein the one or more sensors of the sensor turret includes a camera arranged to capture images of the confined space, and wherein the robotic vehicle further comprises at least one directional light arranged to illuminate the confined space, wherein the robotic vehicle is configured such that the light is never directed in the same direction as the camera, whereby to substantially prevent glare from the light in the images captures from the camera. 24. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 23, wherein the light is provided on the sensor turret and is mounted for rotation with the camera. 25. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 24, wherein the sensor turret further comprises a cover above the at least one light whereby to substantially prevent direct illumination of an upper surface of the confined space by the at least one light. 26. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising: a spray gun mounted to the chassis and arranged to spray a material on a surface spaced from the robotic vehicle; and a sensor mounted to the chassis, the sensor being a directional sensor responsive to an environment of the sensor and for outputting sensor data, 

 wherein the sensor is mounted for motorised movement between a first position for capturing data indicative of a property of the spray gun, a second position for capturing further data indicative of a property of the robotic vehicle, and a third position where the sensor is protected by a cover portion of the robotic vehicle. 27. A robotic vehicle for spraying a material on a surface, the robotic vehicle comprising: a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the robotic vehicle for movement in the longitudinal direction and comprising a plurality of driven wheels arranged for rotation about an axis of rotation substantially transverse with the longitudinal direction to cause movement of the robotic vehicle in the longitudinal direction; a spray gun mounted to the chassis and arranged to spray a material on a surface spaced from the robotic vehicle; a sensor mounted to the chassis, the sensor being a directional sensor responsive to an environment of the sensor and for outputting sensor data, wherein the sensor is mounted for motorised movement between a first position for capturing data indicative of a property of the spray gun, a second position for capturing further data indicative of a property of the robotic vehicle, and a third position where the sensor is protected by a cover portion of the robotic vehicle. 28. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 26 or claim 27, wherein the motorised movement is rotational movement. 29. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 28, wherein the sensor is comprised in a rotatable sensor drum, and wherein the sensor drum is rotatable about an axis substantially parallel to the axis of rotation of the driven wheels. 30. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any of claims 26 to 29, further comprising a controller configured to control the sensor drum to be in the third position during operation of the spray gun. 31. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any of claims 26 to 30, wherein the sensor is at least one of a camera and a depth sensor. 32. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any of claims 26 to 31 , wherein the spray gun comprises a material input connector to connect to a supply hose supplying the material from the rear of the robotic vehicle and wherein the robotic vehicle further comprises a hose support member provided rearwards of the spray gun to support the supply hose away from the vehicle, wherein the hose support member is 

 rotatable about an axis substantially transverse to the longitudinal direction and to the axis of rotation of the driven wheels, whereby to facilitate movement of the supply hose away from a rear of the robotic vehicle during rearward movement of the robotic vehicle. 33. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the chassis further comprises an attachment point for attachment of a harness to the chassis, the harness for pulling the robotic vehicle out of the confined space to remove the robotic vehicle from the confined space, wherein the harness is arranged for tool- less attachment to the attachment point. 34. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 33, wherein the tool-less attachment of the harness to the attachment point is a magnetic attachment. 35. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the robotic vehicle is a driven vehicle, and wherein each wheel comprises a propulsion unit. 36. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the chassis of the robotic vehicle has defined therein a finger groove for removal of the robotic vehicle from the confined space by hand. 37. A robotic vehicle as claimed in claim 36, wherein the finger groove is located at the rear of the robotic vehicle. 38. A robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the robotic vehicle is for insertion and removal from an operating space through a restricted opening, having an extent less than an extent of the assembled robotic vehicle. 39. A wheel for use with the robotic vehicle as claimed in any preceding claim, and comprising a second part of the release mechanism to be released from the first part of the release mechanism provided on the chassis of the robotic vehicle on operation of the release mechanism. 40. A method of removing a robotic vehicle from a confined space, such as under a floor of a building, for example a house, the robotic vehicle comprising: a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the robotic vehicle for movement in the longitudinal direction; a plurality of first parts for a respective plurality of release mechanisms, the first parts each connected to the chassis and each release mechanism for securing a respective wheel to the chassis, 

 wherein each first part provides a mounting point for the respective wheel away from the front or the rear of the chassis, and wherein each release mechanism is operable by an operator to release the respective wheel from the chassis for separate removal of the wheels and the robotic vehicle from the confined space, the method comprising: providing the robotic vehicle in the confined space; operating each release mechanism, whereby to remove each of the driven wheels from the chassis; and removing the chassis of the robotic vehicle from the confined space separately from the driven wheels of the robotic vehicle. 41. A method as claimed in claim 40, wherein the robotic vehicle further comprises a sensor turret comprising one or more sensors responsive to an environment of the robotic vehicle, the sensor turret being secured to the chassis and rotatable about an axis substantially transverse to the longitudinal direction and to an axis of rotation of the wheels, wherein the sensor turret is secured to the chassis by a turret release mechanism operable by an operator, whereby to release the sensor turret from the chassis, and wherein the method further comprises: operating the turret release mechanism, whereby to remove the sensor turret from the chassis; and removing the sensor turret of the robotic vehicle from the confined space separately from the chassis of the robotic vehicle. 42. Sensor apparatus for protecting a sensor device in an environment where material has been sprayed, comprising: a housing having a directional sensor responsive to an environment of the sensor and a viewing window defining a viewing plane and a normal axis extending away from the viewing window; a shutter; and at least one actuator configured to drive the shutter in a plane of motion, wherein the plane of motion is substantially parallel to and spaced from the viewing plane in the direction of the normal axis, 

 wherein the shutter is configured to remove spray material adhered to the viewing window when driven in the plane of motion. Sensor apparatus according to claim 42, wherein the shutter is configured to cover the viewing window, in particular to completely cover the viewing window, in at least one position of use. Sensor apparatus according to claim 42 or claim 43, wherein the shutter comprises a bevelled edge configured to remove adhered spray material from the viewing window. Sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 44, wherein the shutter is configured to be driven from a first position substantially covering the viewing window to a second position substantially clear from the viewing window, wherein the housing comprises a cutting edge spaced from the shutter in the direction of the normal axis, and wherein relative motion between the shutter and the cutting edge removes spray material adhered to the shutter. Sensor apparatus for protecting a sensor device in an environment where material has been sprayed, comprising: a housing having a directional sensor responsive to an environment of the sensor and a viewing window defining a viewing plane and a normal axis extending away from the viewing window; a shutter; and at least one actuator configured to drive a shutter in a plane of motion, wherein the plane of motion is substantially parallel to and spaced from the viewing plane in the direction of the normal axis, wherein the shutter is configured to be driven from a first position substantially covering the viewing window to a second position substantially clear from the viewing window, wherein the housing comprises a cutting edge spaced from the shutter in the direction of the normal axis, and wherein relative motion between the shutter and the cutting edge removes spray material adhered to the shutter. Sensor apparatus according to claim 45 or claim 46, wherein the housing comprises a shroud having defined therein a viewing port, and wherein an edge of the shroud which defines the viewing port provides the cutting edge. Sensor apparatus according to claim 47, wherein the viewing port is formed within a recess in the shroud. 49. Sensor apparatus according to claim 48, wherein the recess is formed of a plurality of angled surfaces, wherein each of the plurality of angled surfaces forms an obtuse angle with the viewing window. 50. Sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 49, wherein the shutter comprises a cleaning member arranged to clean the viewing window when the shutter is driven in the plane of motion. 51. Sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 50, wherein the shutter comprises an anti-adhesive coating. 52. Sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 51 , wherein the sensor is at least one of a camera and a depth sensor. 53. Sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 52, wherein the actuator is driven by a compressed air source. 54. Spraying apparatus comprising a spray gun arranged to spray a material on a surface and sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 53. 55. Spraying apparatus according to claim 53 and 54, wherein the spray gun and actuator are driven by the compressed air source. 56. A robotic vehicle for operating in a confined space, such as under a floor of a building, for example a house, the robotic vehicle comprising a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the robotic vehicle configured for movement in the longitudinal direction; and sensor apparatus according to any of claims 42 to 53 mounted to the chassis. 57. A robotic vehicle for operating in a confined space, such as under a floor of a building, for example a house, the robotic vehicle comprising a chassis having a front and a rear defining a longitudinal direction extending between the front and the rear, the robotic vehicle configured for movement in the longitudinal direction; and spraying apparatus according to claim 54 or claim 55. 58. A robotic vehicle according to claim 56 or claim 57, further comprising a controller configured to drive the shutter prior to movement of the robotic vehicle in the longitudinal direction. 59. Sensor apparatus for protecting a sensor device in an environment where material has been sprayed, comprising: a housing having a directional sensor responsive to an environment of the sensor and a viewing window defining a viewing plane and a normal axis extending away from the viewing window; 

 a transparent film located over the viewing window and fed from a first reel of transparent film to a second reel; and a motor configured to drive the second reel, whereby to replace a portion of transparent film located over the viewing window with clean transparent film from the first reel.
